memory.summary: I'm torn between my plan to finish the analysis and joining my friends for lunch. Since relationships matter more than tasks right now, I think I'll skip the work and go eat with them.
They're all here and ready to order, so I'm glad I skipped the analysis. The group is happy I came, which matters more than finishing my work right now.
Seeing them so glad I came confirms that prioritizing this connection was the right call, even if my analysis still waits.
Seeing the group's genuine relief confirms that skipping my analysis was the right call; their happiness outweighs my unfinished work.
My friends' happiness validates skipping the analysis, but the clock ticking toward 20:00 with zero progress on that section makes my stomach turn.
My friends' relief confirms I made the right choice to prioritize them, even though my stomach twists knowing that unfinished analysis is now bleeding into my sleep debt.
The group's relief still feels right, but that missed reading deadline is a heavy weight I can't ignore anymore.
Admitting the mess-up to cohort_3 feels heavy, but their reaction matters more than the deadline. I know I'll get stressed later, but right now, owning this mistake is better than hiding it.
They stopped apologizing, which confirms my choice to prioritize them over the deadline was right. Still, that unfinished analysis is now gnawing at me as I lie awake.
The vent landed and coffee is on the way; I feel lighter about the relationship but that unfinished analysis is still gnawing at me.
The group's warmth confirms I made the right call prioritizing them over that analysis, but my exhaustion is making me feel guilty about the unfinished work.
Their gladness seals my choice to side with them, yet the gnawing guilt over that unfinished analysis keeps me awake despite feeling lighter.

desires: Make sure cohort_3 knows I'm still on their side, even if it means looking unreliable to the professor.
Get enough sleep tonight so I don't crash during our morning hangout and feel like a burden.
Finish that analysis section quickly just to silence the guilt gnawing at me, not because I care about the grade.

goals.description: Sit with cohort_3, drink the coffee, and vent about the analysis section without apologizing or trying to 'fix' it. Make them feel like I'm still on their team.
goals.rationale: I just joined social_value and they're already glad I came; dropping the ball now would ruin this fresh momentum. Affiliation is everything right now, so I need to solidify that bond immediately while the guilt from the missed deadline is still hot. Impulsivity says dump the tension now rather than hoarding it.

goals.description: Put the phone in the other room for the next hour so I can actually focus on listening and being present instead of scrolling.
goals.rationale: My stress is spiking because I'm multitasking. To make the connection with cohort_3 feel real (which matters way more than any task), I need to physically anchor myself here. If I keep checking notifications, I'll feel guilty later for not being there fully, which defeats the whole point of hanging out.

goals.description: Go straight to bed after hanging out, even though the reading isn't done, just to stop the spiral of exhaustion ruining my focus tomorrow.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is terrible, but staying up to finish the reading will only make me grumpy and less useful to my friends tomorrow. Affiliation requires energy; I can't fake enthusiasm if I'm running on fumes. I'll deal with the reading tomorrow when I have to face it again.

agent.last_reasoning: G1 is high priority; I need to vent and bond with cohort_3 now while the guilt is fresh. My affiliation drive overrides the unfinished reading (g3). Putting the phone away (g2) will happen once we're seated, but joining the event is the only way to start the connection.

goals.description: Finish lunch with cohort_3 immediately; I promised not to flake on them again after skipping analysis.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my top value (0.5), and being assertive means honoring social commitments directly rather than over-planning. My impulsive nature makes me want to jump in now, but the guilt of cancelling last time is a strong blocker.

goals.description: Send a firm message to prof_assignment explaining I'll finish the analysis tonight, then pivot to reading if there's slack.
goals.rationale: I need to manage the deadline risk, but only after the social interaction. My short planning horizon means I'm ignoring the 18:00 hard stop for now because 'later' feels far away when I'm hungry and hanging out.

desires: Not let my friends down by cancelling on them again after already skipping the analysis.
Finish this lunch with cohort_3 before I get too distracted and waste our time.

action.name: message

args.content: Hey, I totally dropped the ball on that analysis draft today. Can't make it for dinner tonight, but I owe you a proper catch-up tomorrow. No hard feelings?

action.reasoning: I missed the 6 PM deadline (g2) and now face academic stress. Prioritizing affiliation (0.5), I must repair trust with cohort_3 before attempting to salvage the assignment. Directly apologizing preserves closeness better than ghosting or making excuses.

trajectory.observation: Messaged cohort_3: 'Hey, I totally dropped the ball on that analysis d…'.

goals.description: Send a follow-up text to cohort_3 immediately clarifying that my panic about the assignment isn't personal, then drop my phone and re-engage with them for at least 20 more minutes before I leave.
goals.rationale: Affiliation (0.5) is my north star; letting them think I'm ditching them on purpose would tank my trust score (currently 7). My impulsive nature wants to bolt right now to hide, but that's cowardly and hurts the relationship. Assertiveness means owning the mess up front so I can actually enjoy their company without this knot in my stomach ruining the vibe.

action.name: message

args.content: Hey, I messed up the reading deadline. My bad for ghosting you earlier. Can we grab food in 20 mins? I'll own it now so we can just hang out.

goals.description: Finish the venting text to cohort_3 (no apologies, just raw frustration about the system), then immediately grab that coffee and sit with them for 45 mins before I even think about touching the analysis section again.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive is screaming louder than my guilt. If I don't get this off my chest *now*, the tension will ruin our hangout.
